{"id":"daily_dashboard-frq","title":"Harden Go backend HTTP layer","description":"Create shared internal packages to eliminate boilerplate and inconsistency across handlers and services:
1. internal/response/ package — WriteJSON(w, status, v) and WriteError(w, status, msg) helpers to replace repeated json.NewEncoder patterns and silent encode-error discards across all handlers
2. internal/httpclient/ or service-level helper — shared pattern for external API calls (set headers, io.LimitReader body, unmarshal JSON) currently duplicated across weather, news, stocks, commute, sunrise, quotes services

{"id":"daily_dashboard-0gm","title":"Fix AssignLabel SQL: ON CONFLICT clause incompatible with partial unique index","description":"AssignLabel repository method uses 'ON CONFLICT(task_id, label_id) DO UPDATE' but the unique constraint on task_label_assignments is a partial unique index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) created separately, not an inline UNIQUE constraint in CREATE TABLE. SQLite's ON CONFLICT (columns) clause only recognizes PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraints defined in the table definition, not standalone CREATE UNIQUE INDEX. Result: every POST /api/tasks/{id}/labels returns 500. {"id":"argus-miv","title":"Notification Center: Webhook handler (Phase 2d)","description":"Fourth sub-task of the backend phase (argus-5a6). Implements HMAC-validated webhook receiver and GitHub event parser. Security-critical — deserves isolated review. Depends on Phase 2c (notification service must exist to create notifications).

Files to create:
- backend/internal/service/webhook.go — ValidateHMACSignature(secret, payload, sigHeader) bool; ParseGitHubEvent(eventType, payload) (*NotificationCreate, error). Event mapping: pull_request (opened/closed+merged/closed) → pr_opened/pr_merged/pr_closed; issue_comment created on PR → pr_comment; pull_request_review_comment created → pr_review_comment. Returns nil for unhandled events (no notification created).
- backend/internal/handler/webhooks.go — AddRoutes registers: POST /api/webhooks/github on PUBLIC router (not requireAuth). Reads X-Hub-Signature-256, X-GitHub-Event, X-GitHub-Delivery headers. Looks up watched repo by owner+repo from payload. Validates HMAC. Parses event. Creates notification via NotificationService. POST /api/webhooks/github/_simulate — only registered when AppEnv == 'development'; skips HMAC validation.
- backend/internal/handler/webhooks_dto.go — GitHubWebhookPayload (pull_request, issue_comment, pull_request_review_comment shapes); SimulatePayload for dev endpoint

Key rules (SECURITY CRITICAL):
- HMAC validation uses crypto/hmac + crypto/sha256; constant-time comparison via hmac.Equal
- Look up webhook secret from user_watched_repos by (owner, repo); decrypt before HMAC comparison
- Return 200 OK even for unhandled events (GitHub retries on non-2xx)
- Return 400 only for malformed JSON; 401 for invalid signature
- _simulate endpoint MUST check AppEnv == 'development' before registering; never active in production
- Store X-GitHub-Delivery as github_delivery_id; duplicate delivery (UNIQUE constraint violation) → return 200 silently
- All logging via log/slog

{"description":"Implement the NSQ FeedFanoutConsumer and the user_feed materialized table that backs it. This phase delivers the write-time fan-out pipeline so the feed read path becomes a cheap indexed scan with a live-join fallback.

DB MIGRATION:
- New table: user_feed (id TEXT PK, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, post_id TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL)
- Indexes: (user_id, created_at DESC, id DESC) for cursor pagination; UNIQUE (user_id, post_id) to prevent duplicates
- FK: post_id → posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE so deleting a post prunes feed rows automatically

FEEDFANOUTCONSUMER (internal/events/feed_fanout.go):
- Topic(): 'post.created', Channel(): 'feed-fanout'
- process(body): unmarshal EventEnvelope → PostCreatedPayload, look up all followers of the author via FollowStore, bulk-insert into user_feed (INSERT OR IGNORE to handle duplicates)
- Log errors per-follower but continue; swallow partial failures gracefully
- Unit tested via process() directly with raw JSON — no NSQ in CI

FEED READ PATH (internal/service/feed.go + repository):
- Primary path: SELECT from user_feed JOIN posts WHERE user_feed.user_id = ? ORDER BY user_feed.created_at DESC, user_feed.id DESC with cursor pagination
- Fallback path: existing live JOIN query (used when user_feed has zero rows for the requesting user — covers new accounts and users who followed before fanout was deployed)
- Fallback detection: if user_feed count for user = 0, run the live join; otherwise use materialized rows

WIRING (cmd/server or main.go):
- ConsumerManager only created if NSQ_LOOKUPD_ADDR is set
- cm.Register(&FeedFanoutConsumer{FollowStore: followRepo, FeedStore: feedRepo})
- cm.Start() warn-only on failure (graceful degradation)
- defer cm.Stop()

TESTING:
- process(): valid payload writes correct user_feed rows, malformed JSON returns error, unknown version is handled
- FeedService: returns materialized rows when user_feed populated, falls back to live join when empty

{"id":"argus-2np","title":"Phase 4: NSQ feed fanout consumer","description":"Implement the NSQ FeedFanoutConsumer and the user_feed materialized table that backs it. This phase delivers the write-time fan-out pipeline so the feed read path becomes a cheap indexed scan with a live-join fallback.

DB MIGRATION:
- New table: user_feed (id TEXT PK, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, post_id TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL)
- Indexes: (user_id, created_at DESC, id DESC) for cursor pagination; UNIQUE (user_id, post_id) to prevent duplicates
- FK: post_id → posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE so deleting a post prunes feed rows automatically

FEEDFANOUTCONSUMER (internal/events/feed_fanout.go):
- Topic(): 'post.created', Channel(): 'feed-fanout'
- process(body): unmarshal EventEnvelope → PostCreatedPayload, look up all followers of the author via FollowStore, bulk-insert into user_feed (INSERT OR IGNORE to handle duplicates)
- Log errors per-follower but continue; swallow partial failures gracefully
- Unit tested via process() directly with raw JSON — no NSQ in CI

FEED READ PATH (internal/service/feed.go + repository):
- Primary path: SELECT from user_feed JOIN posts WHERE user_feed.user_id = ? ORDER BY user_feed.created_at DESC, user_feed.id DESC with cursor pagination
- Fallback path: existing live JOIN query (used when user_feed has zero rows for the requesting user — covers new accounts and users who followed before fanout was deployed)
- Fallback detection: if user_feed count for user = 0, run the live join; otherwise use materialized rows

WIRING (cmd/server or main.go):
- ConsumerManager only created if NSQ_LOOKUPD_ADDR is set
- cm.Register(&FeedFanoutConsumer{FollowStore: followRepo, FeedStore: feedRepo})
- cm.Start() warn-only on failure (graceful degradation)
- defer cm.Stop()

TESTING:
- process(): valid payload writes correct user_feed rows, malformed JSON returns error, unknown version is handled
- FeedService: returns materialized rows when user_feed populated, falls back to live join when empty
